name: away_occupancy_cool_mode_setback
description: Hot day in cool mode: away setback must use setback_cool (79F), not setback_heat (61F)
notes: Before Issue #222 fix, _handle_occupancy_away() applied setback_heat when hvac_mode was 'cool',
sending the thermostat to 61F — far below the comfort floor — instead of the correct setback_cool (79F).
This scenario FAILS before the fix and passes after.
Also verifies the setpoint_consistent_with_mode guard: a cool-mode setpoint must not fall below comfort_heat.

events.note: Morning classification — hot day, HVAC in cool mode targeting comfort_cool (74F)
Baseline reading: indoor 76F, outdoor 82F — confirms hot-day conditions
User leaves home — should apply setback_cool (79F) because HVAC is in cool mode

assertions.reason: Away setback should use setback_cool (79F) when HVAC is in cool mode, not setback_heat (61F)
Setpoint must not be below comfort_heat (70F) when HVAC is in cool mode — a 61F setpoint would be nonsensical and damage comfort on return

verdict.summary: Away setback in cool mode applies setback_cool (79F), not setback_heat (61F)
verdict.observed_behavior: FAILS before fix: setback_applied emits setback_heat (61F) instead of setback_cool (79F); setpoint_consistent_with_mode also fails
